chunks.title: BFD
chunks.text: BFD. Bidirectional Forwarding Detection for sub-second link and path failure detection -- provides a lightweight, protocol-independent liveness check between adjacent or multi-hop forwarding peers. The BFD session is configured on both ends but never leaves the Down state. bfdd is sending Control pa

chunks.title: BGP-4
chunks.text: BGP-4. Border Gateway Protocol version 4 -- path-vector EGP for inter-AS routing and NLRI exchange. bgpd cannot complete the TCP three-way handshake to the peer on port 179. The session oscillates between CONNECT and ACTIVE states indefinitely. In SONiC this is the most common BGP failure and almost

chunks.title: LACP
chunks.text: LACP. Link Aggregation Control Protocol for dynamic LAG/PortChannel negotiation -- enables multiple physical links to be combined into a single logical PortChannel for increased bandwidth and redundancy. teamd is sending LACPDUs but receiving none from the partner. The actor-timeout fires and the po

chunks.title: LLDP
chunks.text: LLDP. Link Layer Discovery Protocol for neighbor and topology discovery -- advertises device identity, capabilities, and port information to directly connected neighbors over Ethernet. The switch is running but 'show lldp table' or 'show lldp neighbors' returns no entries. lldpd may not be running,

chunks.title: MC-LAG
chunks.text: MC-LAG. Multi-Chassis Link Aggregation for dual-homed server/switch redundancy -- two switches act as a single logical LAG endpoint, providing active-active redundancy without spanning tree blocking. The portchannel connecting the two MC-LAG peers (the peer link) has gone down while the ICCP keepali

chunks.title: OSPFv2
chunks.text: OSPFv2. Link-state interior gateway protocol for intra-AS routing with area hierarchy. Two OSPF routers on the same segment have different Hello Interval or Dead Interval values configured. When Hello packets are received, the intervals in the received Hello are compared against the local interface

chunks.title: Static Routing
chunks.text: Static Routing. Manually configured routes for default gateways, stub networks, and policy routing. The static route is visible in 'show ip route static' (kernel RIB) and in APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE, but traffic is still dropped because the route was never programmed into the ASIC forwarding table. The f

chunks.title: STP/RSTP
chunks.text: STP/RSTP. Spanning Tree Protocol for loop prevention in Layer 2 networks. A switch other than the intended root bridge wins the root election because its Bridge ID (priority + MAC) is numerically lower than the configured root. In SONiC environments, the default bridge priority of 32768 means the sw

chunks.title: VXLAN/EVPN
chunks.text: VXLAN/EVPN. Virtual Extensible LAN with EVPN control plane for scalable overlay networking. The VXLAN tunnel source IP address (typically a loopback) is either not configured in CONFIG_DB VXLAN_TUNNEL, the source interface does not exist, or the interface is operationally down. Without a reachable s

chunks.title: BGP / FRR Container
chunks.text: BGP / FRR Container. FRR (Free Range Routing) stack plus SONiC integration daemons. Runs the full BGP, OSPF, IS-IS, and static routing control plane. The critical boundary between FRR and SONiC: bgpcfgd (or frrcfgd) translates CONFIG_DB into FRR running config; zebra computes the RIB; fpmsyncd pumps

chunks.title: Database Container
chunks.text: Database Container. Central Redis engine hosting all 15 SONiC databases for inter-container communication. Every other container depends on this one being healthy. Provides the shared state bus (pub/sub + keyspace notifications) that drives the entire SONiC config-to-ASIC pipeline. Without this cont

chunks.title: Platform Monitor Container
chunks.text: Platform Monitor Container. Hardware monitoring container. Polls and manages fans, PSUs, transceivers, temperatures, and LEDs on the physical platform. Bridges vendor-specific platform APIs (sonic-platform Python plugins) with STATE_DB so that the rest of SONiC can observe hardware health without pl

chunks.title: Switch State Service (swss)
chunks.text: Switch State Service (swss). Central orchestration container. Translates application-level config (APPL_DB) into ASIC-level SAI operations (ASIC_DB). Contains orchagent and all *mgrd/*syncd daemons. Acts as the coordination hub between the control plane (FRR, config management) and the forwarding pl

chunks.title: Syncd Container
chunks.text: Syncd Container. SAI-to-vendor-SDK bridge for ASIC programming. Receives SAI API calls from orchagent (via ASIC_DB redis notifications), translates them into vendor-specific SDK calls, and programs the forwarding ASIC. Acts as the sole gateway between the SONiC control plane and physical hardware. O

chunks.title: TeamD Container
chunks.text: TeamD Container. LAG/PortChannel management via Linux teamd with LACP negotiation. Runs one teamd instance per configured PortChannel, manages LACP PDU exchange with remote peers, and keeps APPL_DB LAG_TABLE and LAG_MEMBER_TABLE synchronized so orchagent can program SAI LAG objects. Acts as the brid

chunks.title: ACL table/rule programming to ASIC
chunks.text: ACL table/rule programming to ASIC. Operator runs 'config acl add table ...' or 'acl-loader update full rules.json'. Operator runs 'config acl add table <name> <type>' — config/main.py calls ConfigDBConnector.set_entry('ACL_TABLE', table_name, table_info) writing the new ACL table definition directl

chunks.title: ARP/neighbor resolution to ASIC programming
chunks.text: ARP/neighbor resolution to ASIC programming. Traffic destined to an unresolved next-hop IP address causes the kernel to send an ARP request; kernel installs the resulting neighbor entry after receiving the ARP reply. A packet arrives for an IP with no ARP entry; kernel sends ARP broadcast request on

chunks.title: Generic SONiC config propagation pipeline (overview)
chunks.text: Generic SONiC config propagation pipeline (overview). Any configuration change: SONiC CLI, config reload, or direct CONFIG_DB write. User intent enters the system as a CONFIG_DB write. 'config ...' subcommands call ConfigDBConnector set_entry/mod_entry on specific tables; 'config reload' replays all

chunks.title: Hardware-learned MAC address propagation to SONiC databases
chunks.text: Hardware-learned MAC address propagation to SONiC databases. ASIC hardware-learns a source MAC address on a bridge port (first packet from unknown source in a VLAN). ASIC hardware-learns source MAC on ingress bridge port. Vendor SAI fires the registered on_fdb_event callback with SAI_FDB_EVENT_LEARN

chunks.title: LAG creation and member add to ASIC
chunks.text: LAG creation and member add to ASIC. 'config portchannel add PortChannel01' followed by 'config portchannel member add PortChannel01 EthernetX'. User runs 'config portchannel add PortChannel01'. add_portchannel() validates the name and writes an entry to CONFIG_DB PORTCHANNEL table with fields admin

chunks.title: Port configuration change to ASIC (speed/MTU/admin state)
chunks.text: Port configuration change to ASIC (speed/MTU/admin state). Operator runs 'config interface speed|mtu|startup|shutdown <ifname> ...'. For speed and MTU: 'config interface speed/mtu' delegates to the portconfig script which calls ConfigDBConnector.mod_entry() on the PORT table. For admin state: 'confi

chunks.title: BGP learned route installation to ASIC
chunks.text: BGP learned route installation to ASIC. BGP session receives UPDATE with new prefix. Receives BGP UPDATE message, runs best-path selection algorithm, installs winning route into FRR RIB. Receives route from bgpd via internal ZAPI, installs into zebra RIB, programs Linux kernel via netlink, and sends

chunks.title: Static route installation to ASIC
chunks.text: Static route installation to ASIC. Operator runs 'config route add prefix <A.B.C.D/M> nexthop <A.B.C.D>' on SONiC CLI. Parses 'config route add' command via click, calls cli_sroute_to_config() to convert CLI args to CONFIG_DB key/fields, then calls config_db.set_entry('STATIC_ROUTE', key, route) to

chunks.title: BGP route withdrawal from ASIC
chunks.text: BGP route withdrawal from ASIC. BGP UPDATE withdraws a prefix or the BGP session goes down. Receives BGP UPDATE message containing a withdrawn prefix (or detects session drop), removes the path_info from the adj-RIB-in, and calls bgp_process() to trigger a new best-path selection. With no remaining

chunks.title: VLAN creation and member assignment to ASIC
chunks.text: VLAN creation and member assignment to ASIC. User runs 'config vlan add <vid>' and 'config vlan member add <vid> <port>'. User runs 'config vlan add 100'. CLI validates the VID range (2-4094), checks for duplicates, then calls config_db.set_entry('VLAN', 'Vlan100', {'vlanid': '100'}) via ValidatedCo

chunks.title: VXLAN VTEP creation to ASIC
chunks.text: VXLAN VTEP creation to ASIC. User runs 'config vxlan add <name> <src_ip>' to configure a VXLAN tunnel endpoint. User runs 'config vxlan add <vtep_name> <src_ip>'. The add_vxlan() click command validates the source IP and interface name, enforces single-VTEP policy, then writes VXLAN_TUNNEL entry to

chunks.title: Direct FRR configuration via docker exec bgp vtysh bypassing bgpcfgd
chunks.text: Direct FRR configuration via docker exec bgp vtysh bypassing bgpcfgd. Engineer connects directly to the FRR daemon inside the bgp container using 'docker exec -it bgp vtysh' and issues configuration commands (router bgp, neighbor, route-map, prefix-list, etc.) instead of using the SONiC config CLI o

chunks.title: Direct redis-cli write to CONFIG_DB or APPL_DB bypassing mgrd validation
chunks.text: Direct redis-cli write to CONFIG_DB or APPL_DB bypassing mgrd validation. Engineer uses 'redis-cli -n 4 HSET ...' (CONFIG_DB) or 'redis-cli -n 0 HSET ...' (APPL_DB) to write entries directly into Redis, bypassing the SONiC config CLI, sonic-cfggen, and the *mgrd daemon validation layer. Often done t

chunks.title: Force-installing Debian packages on SONiC, breaking the immutable image model
chunks.text: Force-installing Debian packages on SONiC, breaking the immutable image model. Operator installs software directly on the SONiC host OS using 'apt-get install', 'dpkg -i', 'dpkg --force-all -i', or 'pip install' (including inside containers via 'docker exec'). Packages are written to the overlay fil

chunks.title: Running express-reboot on a non-Cisco-8000 platform
chunks.text: Running express-reboot on a non-Cisco-8000 platform. Engineer runs 'express-reboot' on a Broadcom, Mellanox/NVIDIA, or Barefoot platform expecting sub-second downtime, not realizing it is only supported on Cisco 8000 hardware. The express-reboot script checks the platform and rejects the command on

chunks.title: Manual edit of config_db.json bypassing ConfigDB
chunks.text: Manual edit of config_db.json bypassing ConfigDB. User edited /etc/sonic/config_db.json directly with a text editor (vi, nano, vim, sed, etc.) instead of using the config CLI, sonic-cfggen, or the REST/gNMI API. The file was not reloaded via 'config reload' after editing, so the live CONFIG_DB Redis

chunks.title: Using ip link/ip addr commands directly instead of SONiC config CLI
chunks.text: Using ip link/ip addr commands directly instead of SONiC config CLI. Operator uses Linux kernel networking commands (ip link set, ip addr add, ip addr del, ip route add, ifconfig) directly on the SONiC host OS to configure interfaces, addresses, or routes instead of using the SONiC config CLI ('conf

chunks.title: Restarting swss container while production traffic is flowing
chunks.text: Restarting swss container while production traffic is flowing. Operator runs 'systemctl restart swss' (or 'docker restart swss') on a production switch carrying live traffic -- often to apply a configuration change, recover from a perceived daemon hang, or follow a stale runbook -- without understan

chunks.title: Stale warmboot files from a previous failed reboot causing boot-up to use outdated state
chunks.text: Stale warmboot files from a previous failed reboot causing boot-up to use outdated state. A previous warm/express/fast reboot failed or was interrupted. The checkpoint files (dump.rdb, sai-warmboot.bin) were saved but the reboot did not complete. On the next reboot attempt, the boot-up process finds

chunks.title: Restarting FRR processes incorrectly, breaking bgpcfgd synchronization
chunks.text: Restarting FRR processes incorrectly, breaking bgpcfgd synchronization. Operator kills or restarts individual FRR daemons directly -- using 'kill $(pidof bgpd)', 'pkill zebra', 'docker exec bgp supervisorctl restart bgpd', or 'docker exec bgp kill -9 <pid>' -- instead of restarting the bgp container

chunks.title: Restarting SONiC containers in wrong dependency order
chunks.text: Restarting SONiC containers in wrong dependency order. Operator restarts one or more SONiC containers without respecting the startup dependency chain. Common patterns: restarting the 'database' container while swss, syncd, bgp, teamd, lldp, or pmon are still running; restarting 'swss' before 'syncd'

chunks.title: BFD session flapping
chunks.text: BFD session flapping. BFD session cycling between Up and Down states repeatedly. How frequently is the BFD session flapping (check syslog for BFD state transitions)?. Is the control plane CPU overloaded? (bfdd is missing keepalives due to scheduling starvation). CPU starvation causing BFD keepalive

chunks.title: BGP session not establishing
chunks.text: BGP session not establishing. BGP peer stuck in Idle/Active/Connect state. What state is the BGP session in?. Does the BGP session show 'Idle (Admin)' or just 'Idle'?. BGP neighbor is administratively shut down. Check CONFIG_DB for shutdown state: redis-cli -n 4 HGETALL 'BGP_NEIGHBOR|<peer_ip>'. If

chunks.title: Express reboot failure
chunks.text: Express reboot failure. Express reboot failed, aborted, or switch came up in degraded state after express reboot. At what phase did the failure occur?. What error message was shown when express-reboot was rejected?. Express reboot is only supported on Cisco 8000 platforms. Check platform: show platf

chunks.title: LAG/PortChannel not forming
chunks.text: LAG/PortChannel not forming. PortChannel members not aggregating or LACP not negotiating. Is the PortChannel configured in CONFIG_DB?. Are member ports assigned to the PortChannel in CONFIG_DB?. Is the teamd process running for this PortChannel (teamdctl shows state)?. Are LACPDUs being sent on memb

chunks.title: Interface/port down
chunks.text: Interface/port down. Interface shows operationally down or no link detected. Is the port administratively enabled (admin_status = up)?. Is a transceiver detected in the port (EEPROM readable)?. Port is administratively shut down (admin_status = down). Enable the port with: config interface startup <

chunks.title: Route missing from hardware forwarding table
chunks.text: Route missing from hardware forwarding table. Route visible in control plane (show ip route) but traffic not being forwarded. Is the route present in the kernel FIB?. Is the route present in APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE?. Is the route programmed in ASIC_DB (orchagent has created a SAI route entry)?. Is the n

chunks.title: VLAN not programming into ASIC
chunks.text: VLAN not programming into ASIC. VLAN configured but traffic not forwarding on VLAN members. Is the VLAN present in CONFIG_DB?. Are VLAN members configured in CONFIG_DB?. VLAN is not configured in CONFIG_DB. The VLAN does not exist at the config layer. Create it with: config vlan add <id>. Then add m

chunks.title: Configuration Reload
chunks.text: Configuration Reload. Reload configuration from /etc/sonic/config_db.json into running CONFIG_DB (Redis), restarting affected services. Backup current running config. Validate the config file to be loaded. Execute config reload. Verify services recovered

chunks.title: Container Restart
chunks.text: Container Restart. Safely restart a specific SONiC Docker container with impact assessment. Identify the target container and confirm it is the one causing issues. Assess container criticality and determine forwarding impact using the impact reference table. Capture pre-restart state snapshot for po

chunks.title: Express Reboot
chunks.text: Express Reboot. Perform a sub-second data plane downtime reboot on Cisco 8000 platforms by skipping PHY init and deferring NPU programming until config is restored. Verify platform is Cisco 8000. Check critical container health. Check for stale warmboot state from previous failed reboot. Verify disk

chunks.title: SONiC Image Upgrade
chunks.text: SONiC Image Upgrade. Upgrade SONiC OS image to a new version with rollback capability. Verify current image version and available disk space. Capture pre-upgrade baseline (BGP, routes, interfaces). Save running configuration to disk. Download or copy the new SONiC image to the switch. Verify new ima

chunks.title: Warm Reboot
chunks.text: Warm Reboot. Reboot with minimal traffic disruption by preserving forwarding state in ASIC. Verify platform supports warm reboot and check system readiness. Check for stale warmboot files and state from a previous failed warm reboot. Enable warm restart if not already enabled. Verify BGP graceful-re

chunks.title: Always use the SONiC config CLI or API for configuration changes
chunks.text: Always use the SONiC config CLI or API for configuration changes. Never edit /etc/sonic/config_db.json directly while SONiC is running. Use 'config' CLI commands for individual changes, 'config load' for bulk changes from a validated file, or 'config replace' (202205+) for full config replacement. F

chunks.title: Always backup config before changes
chunks.text: Always backup config before changes. Before any config change: 'sudo sonic-cfggen -d --print-data > /tmp/config_backup_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).json'. This captures the running CONFIG_DB state. For full system backup, also save FRR config: 'docker exec bgp vtysh -c "show running-config" > /tmp/frr_bac

chunks.title: Understand config save vs config reload
chunks.text: Understand config save vs config reload. 'config save' writes running CONFIG_DB (Redis) to /etc/sonic/config_db.json. 'config reload' reads config_db.json from disk and loads it into Redis, restarting affected services. Order matters: if you made changes and want to keep them across reboot, 'config

chunks.title: Never configure FRR directly via vtysh in production
chunks.text: Never configure FRR directly via vtysh in production. SONiC manages FRR through bgpcfgd (or frrcfgd), which translates CONFIG_DB entries into FRR commands via vtysh. If you configure FRR directly via 'docker exec bgp vtysh', those changes will be overwritten on bgpcfgd restart, config reload, or sys

chunks.title: Understand the impact of container restarts
chunks.text: Understand the impact of container restarts. Container restart impact hierarchy (most to least disruptive): syncd (full ASIC reprogram, traffic loss) > swss (ASIC_DB reprogramming, brief traffic disruption) > bgp (routing reconvergence, possible traffic shift) > teamd (LAG flap) > lldp/snmp (control

chunks.title: Key health check commands for daily operations
chunks.text: Key health check commands for daily operations. Essential monitoring commands: 'show system-health summary' (overall health), 'show interfaces status' (link state), 'show ip bgp summary' (BGP peers), 'show processes cpu' (CPU usage), 'show system-memory' (memory), 'show logging -f' (live syslog). Fo

chunks.title: SONiC upgrade checklist
chunks.text: SONiC upgrade checklist. Pre-upgrade: (1) Backup config: sonic-cfggen -d --print-data > backup.json, (2) Check release notes for breaking changes, (3) Verify disk space: df -h /host, (4) Check current image: sonic-installer list, (5) Plan maintenance window. Upgrade: sonic-installer install <image_u

chunks.title: Where to find logs for each subsystem
chunks.text: Where to find logs for each subsystem. orchagent: /var/log/swss/orchagent.log. syncd: /var/log/syncd.log. bgpcfgd: journalctl -u bgp. FRR: /var/log/frr/frr.log. fpmsyncd: /var/log/swss/fpmsyncd.log. System: /var/log/syslog. Container supervisor: 'docker exec <container> supervisorctl status'. For re

chunks.title: Safe Redis debugging commands
chunks.text: Safe Redis debugging commands. READ-ONLY Redis commands are safe for debugging: 'redis-cli -n 4 KEYS "BGP_NEIGHBOR|*"' (list CONFIG_DB BGP entries), 'redis-cli -n 0 HGETALL "ROUTE_TABLE|10.0.0.0/24"' (check APPL_DB route), 'redis-cli -n 1 KEYS "ASIC_STATE:SAI_OBJECT_TYPE_ROUTE_ENTRY*"' (check ASIC_D

chunks.title: Use warm restart to minimize traffic disruption
chunks.text: Use warm restart to minimize traffic disruption. SONiC warm restart preserves forwarding state in the ASIC while restarting control plane containers. Enable: 'config warm_restart enable <module>'. Modules: system, swss, bgp, teamd. Check status: 'show warm_restart state'. Caveats: not all platforms

chunks.title: Always restart SONiC containers in dependency order, not individually
chunks.text: Always restart SONiC containers in dependency order, not individually. Restart SONiC containers using 'sudo systemctl' in the correct dependency order: database first, then syncd, then swss, then bgp/teamd/lldp/pmon, waiting for each to become healthy before starting the next. For a full system relo

chunks.title: Restart FRR by restarting the bgp container, never by killing individual daemons
chunks.text: Restart FRR by restarting the bgp container, never by killing individual daemons. To restart FRR on SONiC, always use 'sudo systemctl restart bgp' at the host level so bgpcfgd runs its full initialization, re-reads CONFIG_DB, renders fresh FRR config templates, and calls 'vtysh -f' to load the autho

chunks.title: Remove stale warmboot checkpoint files before initiating any warm or fast reboot
chunks.text: Remove stale warmboot checkpoint files before initiating any warm or fast reboot. Before any warm, express, or fast reboot, verify and remove leftover checkpoint files: 'sudo rm -f /host/warmboot/dump.rdb /host/warmboot/sai-warmboot.bin' and clear warmboot state with 'redis-cli -n 6 DEL WARM_RESTART

chunks.title: Use the SONiC config CLI for all interface and address changes, never iproute2 directly
chunks.text: Use the SONiC config CLI for all interface and address changes, never iproute2 directly. Configure interfaces, IP addresses, and static routes exclusively through the SONiC config CLI ('config interface startup/shutdown', 'config interface ip add/remove', 'config route add') and persist with 'config

chunks.title: Never install packages with apt, dpkg --force, or pip directly on the SONiC host
chunks.text: Never install packages with apt, dpkg --force, or pip directly on the SONiC host. Add tools or libraries to SONiC by building a custom image via sonic-buildimage; never use 'apt-get install', 'dpkg -i', 'dpkg --force-all', or 'pip install' on the host or inside running containers. Packages installed

chunks.title: Verify platform support before choosing a reboot type
chunks.text: Verify platform support before choosing a reboot type. Always run 'show platform summary' before selecting a reboot type. Express-reboot is supported only on Cisco 8000 hardware, which provides the SDK features required for sub-second data plane downtime. On Broadcom, NVIDIA/Mellanox, or Barefoot pl

chunks.title: Create the VLAN before adding a VXLAN tunnel map entry
chunks.text: Create the VLAN before adding a VXLAN tunnel map entry. Always run 'config vlan add <id>' and confirm the VLAN exists in STATE_DB before issuing 'config vxlan map add'. vxlanmgrd's doVxlanTunnelMapCreateTask() creates a Linux VXLAN netdevice keyed to the VLAN; if the VLAN is absent, netdevice creati

chunks.title: Check TCAM headroom with CRM before deploying ACL rules
chunks.text: Check TCAM headroom with CRM before deploying ACL rules. Run 'crm show resources acl-entry' and 'crm show resources acl-counter' before loading ACL rules via acl-loader. When the TCAM is full, sai_acl_api->create_acl_entry() returns SAI_STATUS_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES and syncd logs SAI_STATUS_TABLE_F

chunks.title: Match speed and MTU on all member ports before adding them to a LAG
chunks.text: Match speed and MTU on all member ports before adding them to a LAG. Before running 'config portchannel member add', confirm that the candidate port's speed and MTU equal those already set on the PortChannel; add_portchannel_member() enforces this check and rejects mismatched ports at the CLI. A mis

chunks.title: Enable warm restart for swss before any planned container restart
chunks.text: Enable warm restart for swss before any planned container restart. Run 'config warm_restart enable swss' and verify with 'show warm_restart config' before restarting the swss container on a production switch. Without warm restart, orchagent sends a SAI switch finalize on exit that flushes all routes

chunks.title: Configure CRM high/low thresholds for all hardware resource types
chunks.text: Configure CRM high/low thresholds for all hardware resource types. Set explicit high and low thresholds in the CRM CONFIG_DB table for routes, neighbors, nexthop groups, ACL entries, ACL tables, and FDB entries. Without configured thresholds, orchagent receives no early warning before SAI_STATUS_TAB

chunks.title: Enable suppress-fib-pending in DEVICE_METADATA before deploying BGP
chunks.text: Enable suppress-fib-pending in DEVICE_METADATA before deploying BGP. Set 'localhost.suppress-fib-pending' to 'enabled' in DEVICE_METADATA before BGP sessions carry production traffic. When disabled (the default), BGP advertises routes to peers immediately after the FRR RIB selects them, before orcha

chunks.title: Validate all MCLAG_DOMAIN fields and PortChannel references before enabling MCLAG
chunks.text: Validate all MCLAG_DOMAIN fields and PortChannel references before enabling MCLAG. Before enabling MCLAG, confirm that 'source_ip' and 'peer_ip' are populated in the MCLAG_DOMAIN CONFIG_DB entry and that 'peer_link' is configured (optional in the YANG schema but required for a functional MCLAG domai

chunks.title: Remove default SNMP community strings and restrict all communities to RO type
chunks.text: Remove default SNMP community strings and restrict all communities to RO type. Delete any default community strings such as 'public' or 'private' from SNMP_COMMUNITY and replace them with site-specific strings set to type 'RO'. The SNMP_COMMUNITY 'TYPE' field accepts 'RO' or 'RW'; an 'RW' community

chunks.title: Use the five-stage pipeline to isolate where a route stops being programmed
chunks.text: Use the five-stage pipeline to isolate where a route stops being programmed. When a route is visible in 'show ip route' but traffic is not forwarded, walk the pipeline in order: FRR RIB ('docker exec bgp vtysh -c show ip route <prefix>'), kernel FIB ('ip route show <prefix>'), APPL_DB ROUTE_TABLE ('

chunks.title: bgpcfgd: template.*error|jinja2.*error|TemplateSyntaxError
chunks.text: template.*error|jinja2.*error|TemplateSyntaxError. bgpcfgd failed to render a Jinja2 template for FRR configuration. Invalid or missing fields in CONFIG_DB BGP tables. Custom template modification broke Jinja2 syntax. bgpcfgd code bug after SONiC upgrade

chunks.title: bgpcfgd: vtysh.*failed|vtysh.*error|vtysh.*command rejected
chunks.text: vtysh.*failed|vtysh.*error|vtysh.*command rejected. bgpcfgd's vtysh command to configure FRR was rejected. FRR (bgpd) rejected the configuration command. Syntax error in generated vtysh command. FRR is not running or crashed

chunks.title: bgpcfgd: CONFIG_DB.*update|neighbor.*added|neighbor.*removed
chunks.text: CONFIG_DB.*update|neighbor.*added|neighbor.*removed. bgpcfgd detected a CONFIG_DB change and is syncing to FRR. Normal operation -- user made a config change via CLI/API

chunks.title: fpmsyncd: FPM.*connected|Connected to.*FPM|fpm_connect.*success
chunks.text: FPM.*connected|Connected to.*FPM|fpm_connect.*success. fpmsyncd successfully established a TCP socket connection to zebra's FPM server; route programming pipeline between FRR and APPL_DB is now active. Normal startup sequence after bgp container starts. zebra restarted and fpmsyncd reconnected. Warm

chunks.title: fpmsyncd: FPM.*disconnect|fpm.*closed|Lost connection.*FPM|FPM socket closed
chunks.text: FPM.*disconnect|fpm.*closed|Lost connection.*FPM|FPM socket closed. fpmsyncd lost its TCP connection to zebra's FPM server; new routes from FRR will not reach APPL_DB or the ASIC until reconnection. zebra process crashed or was restarted. bgp container OOM kill or supervisord restart. FPM port 2620

chunks.title: fpmsyncd: RouteSync.*add|ROUTE_TABLE.*SET|fpmsyncd.*Adding route|addRoute
chunks.text: RouteSync.*add|ROUTE_TABLE.*SET|fpmsyncd.*Adding route|addRoute. fpmsyncd is writing a route entry to ROUTE_TABLE in APPL_DB; this is the normal path for FRR-learned routes entering the SONiC pipeline. BGP session received a new prefix. Static route configured and activated. Warm restart reconciliat

chunks.title: fpmsyncd: RouteSync.*del|ROUTE_TABLE.*DEL|fpmsyncd.*Deleting route|deleteRoute
chunks.text: RouteSync.*del|ROUTE_TABLE.*DEL|fpmsyncd.*Deleting route|deleteRoute. fpmsyncd is removing a route entry from ROUTE_TABLE in APPL_DB; orchagent will subsequently withdraw the route from the ASIC. BGP withdrew the prefix (peer down, policy change, MED change). Static route removed from config. Interf

chunks.title: fpmsyncd: Failed to write.*APPL_DB|redis.*error|APPL_DB.*write.*fail|RedisReply.*error
chunks.text: Failed to write.*APPL_DB|redis.*error|APPL_DB.*write.*fail|RedisReply.*error. fpmsyncd could not write a route to APPL_DB due to a Redis error; routes are silently dropped and will not reach orchagent or the ASIC. redis-server process crashed or restarted mid-write. Redis memory limit hit (maxmemory

chunks.title: fpmsyncd: Failed to parse.*FPM|fpm.*malformed|invalid FPM message|FPM msg.*length
chunks.text: Failed to parse.*FPM|fpm.*malformed|invalid FPM message|FPM msg.*length. fpmsyncd received a FPM netlink message from zebra that it could not parse; the route update is discarded and will not reach APPL_DB. FRR version mismatch between zebra and fpmsyncd -- netlink message format incompatibility. Me

chunks.title: fpmsyncd: reconcil|RouteSync.*warm.*restart|fpmsyncd.*replay|reconciliation.*complet
chunks.text: reconcil|RouteSync.*warm.*restart|fpmsyncd.*replay|reconciliation.*complet. fpmsyncd is performing post-restart route reconciliation -- replaying all routes from zebra to APPL_DB to ensure consistency after a warm restart. Warm restart enabled and bgp container restarted. fpmsyncd restarted while ze

chunks.title: fpmsyncd: invalid.*nexthop|nexthop.*unresolvable|Received route.*invalid.*nh|bad nexthop
chunks.text: invalid.*nexthop|nexthop.*unresolvable|Received route.*invalid.*nh|bad nexthop. fpmsyncd received a route from zebra with a next-hop that cannot be resolved to a valid interface or neighbor; the route is dropped and will not be programmed into the ASIC. Recursive next-hop chain too deep or contains

chunks.title: orchagent: SAI_STATUS.*create_route_entry
chunks.text: SAI_STATUS.*create_route_entry. orchagent failed to program a route into the ASIC via SAI. ASIC FIB/TCAM table full -- check scale limits. SAI library bug or platform-specific constraint. syncd crashed or not running. Invalid next-hop object ID (neighbor not resolved)

chunks.title: orchagent: SAI_STATUS.*create_neighbor_entry
chunks.text: SAI_STATUS.*create_neighbor_entry. orchagent failed to program a neighbor (ARP/ND) entry into the ASIC. Neighbor table full in ASIC. Invalid interface/router interface object. syncd not responding

chunks.title: orchagent: ConsumerStateTable.*timeout|slow consumer
chunks.text: ConsumerStateTable.*timeout|slow consumer. orchagent is falling behind processing APPL_DB notifications. CPU congestion -- too many routes being programmed simultaneously. syncd slow to respond (ASIC programming delay). Memory pressure causing swap

chunks.title: orchagent: Port.*oper.*status.*(up|down)
chunks.text: Port.*oper.*status.*(up|down). Port operational status changed -- link up or down event. Physical link change (cable, transceiver, peer port). Admin shutdown/no-shutdown. Auto-negotiation failure

chunks.title: orchagent: warm.*restart|WarmStart
chunks.text: warm.*restart|WarmStart. orchagent warm restart state change -- reconciliation in progress or complete. Warm restart was initiated via config warm_restart enable. swss container restarted with warm_restart enabled

chunks.title: orchagent: CRM.*exceeded|CRM.*threshold
chunks.text: CRM.*exceeded|CRM.*threshold. Critical Resource Monitoring detected resource usage above threshold. ASIC table approaching capacity (routes, neighbors, ACLs, next-hops). Resource leak in orchagent

chunks.title: portmgrd: Port MTU set to \d+
chunks.text: Port MTU set to \d+. portmgrd successfully applied an MTU value to a kernel network interface via ip link set mtu. Operator applied 'interface mtu <value>' in CONFIG_DB. Automated provisioning pushed a new MTU via RESTAPI or sonic-cfggen

chunks.title: portmgrd: Port admin state.*changed.*(up|down)|ip link set .*(up|down)
chunks.text: Port admin state.*changed.*(up|down)|ip link set .*(up|down). portmgrd applied an admin-up or admin-down to the kernel interface in response to a CONFIG_DB change. Operator ran 'interface shutdown' or 'no shutdown' via CLI or SONiC config. Automated rollout toggling ports during provisioning. Warm r

chunks.title: portmgrd: Failed to set.*MTU|Failed to set port MTU|error.*mtu.*EOPNOTSUPP|EINVAL.*mtu
chunks.text: Failed to set.*MTU|Failed to set port MTU|error.*mtu.*EOPNOTSUPP|EINVAL.*mtu. portmgrd attempted to apply an MTU value to a kernel interface but the kernel or driver rejected it. Requested MTU exceeds the driver maximum for this NIC/ASIC (common above 9216 bytes). Interface is in a bonded/LAG group

chunks.title: portmgrd: Port speed.*applied|speed.*configured|set.*speed.*\d+
chunks.text: Port speed.*applied|speed.*configured|set.*speed.*\d+. portmgrd wrote a new port speed (or autoneg setting) to the kernel interface or notified orchagent via APPL_DB. Operator set interface speed via CLI or config_db.json. Autoneg mode changed alongside speed hint

chunks.title: portmgrd: FEC.*changed|FEC.*configured|set.*fec
chunks.text: FEC.*changed|FEC.*configured|set.*fec. portmgrd applied a Forward Error Correction mode change to the port, writing it to APPL_DB for orchagent/syncd to program into the ASIC. Operator set 'interface fec <mode>' (none, rs, fc) via CLI. Autoneg negotiated a new FEC mode with the peer

chunks.title: portmgrd: Failed to configure port|netlink.*error|RTNETLINK.*error|configure.*port.*failed
chunks.text: Failed to configure port|netlink.*error|RTNETLINK.*error|configure.*port.*failed. portmgrd issued a netlink request to configure the port but received an error response from the kernel. Interface does not exist in the kernel at the time of the netlink call (timing/ordering issue). Kernel returned EN

chunks.title: portmgrd: waiting for port ready|port.*not ready|port state check.*wait
chunks.text: waiting for port ready|port.*not ready|port state check.*wait. portmgrd is polling STATE_DB waiting for orchagent to mark the port as ready before applying further configuration -- a normal but time-bounded wait state. orchagent has not yet created the SAI port object and written 'ok' to STATE_DB:PO

chunks.title: portmgrd: Interface description.*updated|alias.*applied|description.*written
chunks.text: Interface description.*updated|alias.*applied|description.*written. portmgrd applied an interface description or alias to the kernel interface (written to /sys/class/net/<intf>/ifalias or via netlink). Operator set 'interface description <text>' in CONFIG_DB. Bulk provisioning script updated interfa

chunks.title: syncd: SAI_START_TYPE_EXPRESS_BOOT
chunks.text: SAI_START_TYPE_EXPRESS_BOOT. syncd detected express boot mode and is initializing SAI with express warm start (type 4). NPU will NOT be programmed until APPLY_VIEW is received. Normal express reboot boot-up sequence

chunks.title: syncd: SAI_SWITCH_ATTR_FAST_API_ENABLE.*fail|setFastAPIEnable.*fail
chunks.text: SAI_SWITCH_ATTR_FAST_API_ENABLE.*fail|setFastAPIEnable.*fail. syncd failed to set the FAST_API_ENABLE attribute on one or more ASICs during express reboot pre-shutdown. The express reboot will abort. Vendor SAI does not support express boot (non-Cisco-8000 platform). SDK internal error during the at

chunks.title: syncd: syncd_request_shutdown.*-pxe|pre.shutdown.*express
chunks.text: syncd_request_shutdown.*-pxe|pre.shutdown.*express. The express reboot script requested syncd pre-shutdown with the express flag (-pxe). syncd will set FAST_API_ENABLE on all ASICs and serialize state to sai-warmboot.bin. Normal express reboot shutdown sequence

chunks.title: syncd: SAI_REDIS_NOTIFY_SYNCD_APPLY_VIEW|APPLY_VIEW
chunks.text: SAI_REDIS_NOTIFY_SYNCD_APPLY_VIEW|APPLY_VIEW. syncd received the APPLY_VIEW notification from orchagent. This triggers NPU bulk programming in express boot mode. The NPU will now be initialized with the restored configuration. Normal express reboot boot-up -- orchagent finished reconciliation and si

chunks.title: syncd: warm.*boot.*fail|SAI.*warm.*init.*fail|unable.*restore.*state
chunks.text: warm.*boot.*fail|SAI.*warm.*init.*fail|unable.*restore.*state. syncd failed to initialize from sai-warmboot.bin during warm/express boot. The SAI layer could not deserialize the saved ASIC state. The switch will likely fall back to cold boot behavior. sai-warmboot.bin is corrupt or incomplete (disk

chunks.title: syncd: punt.*header.*mismatch|inject.*header.*invalid|packet.*format.*incompatible
chunks.text: punt.*header.*mismatch|inject.*header.*invalid|packet.*format.*incompatible. During the express boot V1-to-V2 transition window (~1-2s), punt/inject packet headers from the old software version are incompatible with the new NPU expectations. Packets may be dropped or corrupted. Cross-version express

chunks.title: syncd: sai_api_initialize.*fail|SAI initialization failed
chunks.text: sai_api_initialize.*fail|SAI initialization failed. syncd failed to initialize the SAI API -- cannot communicate with ASIC. Vendor SAI library (libsai.so) missing or corrupt. ASIC hardware failure or not detected. Kernel driver for ASIC not loaded. Incompatible SAI library version

chunks.title: syncd: SAI_STATUS_FAILURE|SAI_STATUS_NOT_SUPPORTED|SAI_STATUS_TABLE_FULL
chunks.text: SAI_STATUS_FAILURE|SAI_STATUS_NOT_SUPPORTED|SAI_STATUS_TABLE_FULL. SAI API call returned an error status from the vendor ASIC SDK. SAI_STATUS_TABLE_FULL: ASIC table capacity reached. SAI_STATUS_NOT_SUPPORTED: Feature not supported on this platform. SAI_STATUS_FAILURE: Generic vendor SDK error. SAI_S

chunks.title: syncd: FDB.*event|port.*state.*notification|BFD.*session
chunks.text: FDB.*event|port.*state.*notification|BFD.*session. ASIC hardware notification received -- FDB learning event, port state change, or BFD session event. Normal ASIC operation -- hardware events propagated to software. FDB: MAC address learned/aged/moved. Port: Physical link state change detected by PH

chunks.title: daemon: orchagent
chunks.text: orchagent daemon in swss container. Central orchestration agent. Subscribes to APPL_DB tables (routes, neighbors, ports, VLANs, ACLs, tunnels) and translates each desired-state entry into SAI API calls written to ASIC_DB. Syncd consumes ASIC_DB to drive the vendor SDK. Orchagent is the single point

chunks.title: daemon: syncd
chunks.text: syncd daemon in syncd container. SAI-to-SDK bridge. Consumes ASIC_DB entries written by orchagent and calls the vendor SAI implementation (libsai.so) to program the forwarding ASIC. Also polls ASIC counters via the flex-counter framework and publishes them to COUNTERS_DB. Vendor-specific; each platf

chunks.title: daemon: fpmsyncd
chunks.text: fpmsyncd daemon in bgp container. FPM (Forwarding Plane Manager) synchronization daemon. Receives RIB updates from FRR's zebra process via the FPM socket (Unix socket, default port 2620) and writes them as ROUTE_TABLE entries into APPL_DB. Bridges the FRR routing stack and the SONiC forwarding pipel

chunks.title: daemon: bgpcfgd
chunks.text: bgpcfgd daemon in bgp container. BGP configuration daemon. Watches CONFIG_DB for BGP neighbor, peer-group, prefix-list, route-map, and global ASN changes and translates them into FRR vtysh configuration commands applied live. Ensures FRR configuration stays in sync with CONFIG_DB without requiring a

chunks.title: daemon: portsyncd
chunks.text: portsyncd daemon in swss container. Port synchronization daemon. Reads port configuration (speed, MTU, admin state, FEC mode, auto-negotiation) from CONFIG_DB and writes desired state to APPL_DB:PORT_TABLE. Also monitors netlink events for kernel-level link state changes and publishes them to STATE_

chunks.title: daemon: neighsyncd
chunks.text: neighsyncd daemon in swss container. Neighbor synchronization daemon. Monitors the kernel ARP/NDP table via netlink NEIGH events and writes resolved neighbor entries (IP-to-MAC mappings) to APPL_DB:NEIGH_TABLE. Orchagent converts these into SAI neighbor and next-hop objects in ASIC_DB

chunks.title: daemon: portmgrd
chunks.text: portmgrd daemon in swss container. Port manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:PORT table and applies port configuration to the Linux kernel (via netlink/ip commands): sets MTU, admin state (ip link set up/down), and interface description. Counterpart to portsyncd; portmgrd handles the kernel side

chunks.title: daemon: intfmgrd
chunks.text: intfmgrd daemon in swss container. Interface manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:INTERFACE and CONFIG_DB:VLAN_INTERFACE tables and applies IP address assignments to Linux kernel interfaces. Writes interface entries to APPL_DB for orchagent to create the corresponding RIF (Router Interface) SAI o

chunks.title: daemon: vlanmgrd
chunks.text: vlanmgrd daemon in swss container. VLAN manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:VLAN and CONFIG_DB:VLAN_MEMBER tables and creates/destroys Linux VLAN bridge interfaces and bridge FDB entries. Writes VLAN and VLAN member entries to APPL_DB so orchagent can create the corresponding SAI VLAN objects an

chunks.title: daemon: teammgrd
chunks.text: teammgrd daemon in teamd container. Team (LAG/PortChannel) manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:PORTCHANNEL and CONFIG_DB:PORTCHANNEL_MEMBER tables and manages the teamd (libteam) process lifecycle: starts/stops a teamd instance per PortChannel and adds/removes member ports. Writes LAG state to A

chunks.title: daemon: teamsyncd
chunks.text: teamsyncd daemon in teamd container. Team synchronization daemon. Subscribes to LAG state changes from the teamd processes (via libteamdctl) and publishes LAG member port state (selected/deselected by LACP) to APPL_DB:LAG_MEMBER_TABLE. Orchagent uses this to add or remove ports from the SAI LAG obje

chunks.title: daemon: lldpsyncd
chunks.text: lldpsyncd daemon in lldp container. LLDP synchronization daemon. Polls the lldpd process for neighbor information (via lldpcli) and publishes discovered LLDP neighbor entries to APPL_DB:LLDP_ENTRY_TABLE. These entries are used by the topology discovery CLI and optionally by lldpmgrd for interface de

chunks.title: daemon: lldpmgrd
chunks.text: lldpmgrd daemon in lldp container. LLDP manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:DEVICE_METADATA and CONFIG_DB:PORT tables and configures the lldpd process accordingly: sets the chassis ID, system description, and per-port LLDP parameters (admin status, tx/rx enable). Translates SONiC CONFIG_DB inten

chunks.title: daemon: xcvrd
chunks.text: xcvrd daemon in pmon container. Transceiver daemon. Manages pluggable optics and DAC cables: reads EEPROM data (vendor, part number, serial, DOM measurements) from SFP/QSFP/OSFP modules via the platform SFP API and publishes it to STATE_DB:TRANSCEIVER_INFO and STATE_DB:TRANSCEIVER_DOM_SENSOR tables.

chunks.title: daemon: nbrmgrd
chunks.text: nbrmgrd daemon in swss container. Neighbor manager daemon. Manages static ARP/NDP entries and neighbor probing from CONFIG_DB. Writes neighbor entries to kernel via netlink

chunks.title: daemon: buffermgrd
chunks.text: buffermgrd daemon in swss container. Buffer manager daemon. Translates CONFIG_DB buffer pool/profile/PG/queue configuration into APPL_DB entries for orchagent to program SAI buffer objects

chunks.title: daemon: coppmgrd
chunks.text: coppmgrd daemon in swss container. CoPP (Control Plane Policing) manager daemon. Reads COPP_TABLE from CONFIG_DB and writes CoPP trap/policer entries to APPL_DB for orchagent to program in SAI

chunks.title: daemon: tunnelmgrd
chunks.text: tunnelmgrd daemon in swss container. Tunnel manager daemon. Manages VXLAN tunnel configuration from CONFIG_DB and writes tunnel entries to APPL_DB for orchagent VxlanTunnelOrch

chunks.title: daemon: sflowmgrd
chunks.text: sflowmgrd daemon in swss container. sFlow manager daemon. Manages sFlow sampling configuration from CONFIG_DB

chunks.title: daemon: natmgrd
chunks.text: natmgrd daemon in swss container. NAT manager daemon. Manages NAT/NAPT configuration from CONFIG_DB

chunks.title: daemon: macsecmgrd
chunks.text: macsecmgrd daemon in swss container. MACsec manager daemon. Manages MACsec encryption configuration from CONFIG_DB

chunks.title: daemon: bgpd
chunks.text: bgpd daemon in bgp container. FRR BGP daemon. Implements BGP-4 protocol: establishes sessions, exchanges routes, runs best-path selection. Managed by bgpcfgd; do not configure directly via vtysh

chunks.title: daemon: zebra
chunks.text: zebra daemon in bgp container. FRR Zebra daemon. Central RIB manager for FRR. Receives routes from all FRR protocol daemons, selects best route, programs kernel FIB via netlink, and notifies fpmsyncd via FPM socket

chunks.title: daemon: staticd
chunks.text: staticd daemon in bgp container. FRR static route daemon. Manages static routes configured via FRR and installs them into zebra RIB

chunks.title: daemon: frrcfgd
chunks.text: frrcfgd daemon in bgp container. Unified FRR configuration daemon (alternative to bgpcfgd). Event-driven CONFIG_DB subscriber that supports OSPF, VRFs, IGMP, PIM in addition to BGP. Enabled by setting frr_mgmt_framework_config=true in DEVICE_METADATA

chunks.title: daemon: ospfd
chunks.text: ospfd daemon in bgp container. FRR OSPF daemon. Implements OSPFv2 protocol

chunks.title: daemon: ospf6d
chunks.text: ospf6d daemon in bgp container. FRR OSPFv3 daemon. Implements OSPFv3 for IPv6

chunks.title: daemon: isisd
chunks.text: isisd daemon in bgp container. FRR IS-IS daemon. Implements IS-IS protocol

chunks.title: daemon: bfdd
chunks.text: bfdd daemon in bgp container. FRR BFD daemon. Implements Bidirectional Forwarding Detection for sub-second failure detection

chunks.title: daemon: psud
chunks.text: psud daemon in pmon container. Power Supply Unit daemon. Monitors PSU presence, status (OK/NOT OK), input/output voltage, current, and power via the platform PSU API. Publishes PSU operational data to STATE_DB:PSU_INFO table. Triggers syslog alerts on PSU insertion, removal, or failure events

chunks.title: daemon: thermalctld
chunks.text: thermalctld daemon in pmon container. Thermal control daemon. Reads temperature sensors from the platform thermal API and adjusts fan speeds to maintain safe operating temperatures. Publishes thermal data to STATE_DB:TEMPERATURE_INFO and fan data to STATE_DB:FAN_INFO. Implements thermal policies: if

chunks.title: daemon: syseepromd
chunks.text: syseepromd daemon in pmon container. System EEPROM daemon. Reads the chassis EEPROM (TlvInfo format per ONIE specification) at startup and publishes hardware identity data (serial number, MAC address base, platform name, manufacturer) to STATE_DB:EEPROM_INFO table. Runs once at boot; does not poll c

chunks.title: daemon: iccpd
chunks.text: iccpd daemon in iccpd container. Inter-Chassis Communication Protocol daemon. Implements ICCP (RFC 7275) for MC-LAG: establishes a TCP session between MC-LAG peers, synchronizes MAC addresses, ARP/NDP entries, and LAG membership state. Detects peer failures via keepalive mechanism and triggers failo

chunks.title: daemon: mclagsyncd
chunks.text: mclagsyncd daemon in iccpd container. MC-LAG synchronization daemon. Receives synchronized MAC, ARP, and port-channel state from iccpd and writes it to APPL_DB so orchagent can program the ASIC with peer-learned entries. Bridges the ICCP control plane and the SONiC forwarding pipeline

chunks.title: daemon: stpmgrd
chunks.text: stpmgrd daemon in stp container. STP manager daemon. Subscribes to CONFIG_DB:STP, STP_VLAN, STP_PORT, and STP_VLAN_PORT tables (bridge priority, port cost, forward delay, hello time) and relays validated configuration to the stpd process, which runs the actual protocol state machines. Part of the sw

chunks.title: daemon: stpd
chunks.text: stpd daemon in stp container. STP protocol daemon. Implements the STP/RSTP/PVST+ state machines: sends and receives BPDUs via raw sockets, runs port role selection and topology change handling. Receives configuration from stpmgrd and writes port forwarding state (Forwarding/Blocking/Disabled) to APP

chunks.title: daemon: fdbsyncd
chunks.text: fdbsyncd daemon in swss container. FDB synchronization daemon for VXLAN-EVPN. Subscribes to STATE_DB FDB_TABLE (via SubscriberStateTable in fdbsync.cpp) and, when EVPN NVO is configured (CFG_VXLAN_EVPN_NVO_TABLE_NAME), programs learned MACs into the kernel bridge FDB via 'bridge fdb replace' netlink

chunks.title: daemon: gbsyncd
chunks.text: gbsyncd daemon in syncd container. Gearbox synchronization daemon. Manages SAI objects for external PHY/retimer (Gearbox) ASICs. Reads from GB_ASIC_DB and calls the Gearbox SAI implementation to program the external PHY. Only present on platforms with external retimer/PHY devices (e.g., certain Memo

chunks.title: daemon: redis-server
chunks.text: redis-server daemon in database container. Redis database engine. Hosts all 15 SONiC Redis databases (DB 0-14) in a single Redis instance with logical database separation. Provides the pub/sub notification mechanism that all SONiC daemons use for inter-process communication. The database container i

chunks.title: timer: BGP Hold Timer
chunks.text: BGP Hold Timer timer for bgp-4. Use BFD for sub-second failure detection. Hold timer is a BGP-level fallback for environments where BFD is unavailable. Values below 15s are not recommended on high-CPU platforms due to keepalive jitter risk. Negotiated to the lower of the two peers' values

chunks.title: timer: BGP Keepalive Timer
chunks.text: BGP Keepalive Timer timer for bgp-4. Keepalive is always negotiated as hold/3. If you change the hold timer, the keepalive follows proportionally. Mismatched keepalive timers between peers are not an error; BGP uses the negotiated hold timer from OPEN message exchange

chunks.title: timer: BGP Connect-Retry Timer
chunks.text: BGP Connect-Retry Timer timer for bgp-4. Lower values (10–30s) speed up recovery after a peer restart but increase CPU load from connection churn on unstable links. FRR default in SONiC is 10s for most deployments; check running config. Governed by RFC 4271 ConnectRetryTimer

chunks.title: timer: OSPF Hello Interval
chunks.text: OSPF Hello Interval timer for ospfv2. Hello interval must match between neighbors or adjacency will not form. Lowering to 1s with a corresponding dead interval of 4s speeds convergence but increases CPU on large broadcast domains. BFD is preferred for sub-second detection

chunks.title: timer: OSPF Dead Interval
chunks.text: OSPF Dead Interval timer for ospfv2. Dead interval must be at least 4x the hello interval per RFC 2328 (enforced by FRR). Must match between neighbors. Use BFD for faster failure detection rather than tuning dead interval below 4s

chunks.title: timer: OSPF LSA MaxAge
chunks.text: OSPF LSA MaxAge timer for ospfv2. Not configurable in standard OSPF implementations including FRR. LSA refresh occurs at LSRefreshTime (default 1800s) before MaxAge is reached. Do not confuse LSA age with the flooding mechanism

chunks.title: timer: OSPF SPF Delay / Throttle
chunks.text: OSPF SPF Delay / Throttle timer for ospfv2. FRR uses a three-parameter SPF throttle (delay, min-hold, max-hold). Tune with 'timers throttle spf'. Low initial delay (0–50ms) is good for fast convergence; ensure max-hold caps churn under instability

chunks.title: timer: BFD Detection Multiplier
chunks.text: BFD Detection Multiplier timer for bfd. Lower multiplier (2) gives faster detection but is less tolerant of packet loss. Multiplier of 3 is standard; 5 is used on lossy links or high-CPU platforms. Asymmetric multipliers between peers are allowed

chunks.title: timer: BFD Desired Min Tx Interval
chunks.text: BFD Desired Min Tx Interval timer for bfd. 300ms is the recommended starting point for software BFD. For hardware-offloaded BFD on supported ASICs (Broadcom DNX, Mellanox Spectrum), 50–100ms is achievable. Sub-50ms requires hardware BFD support. Coordinate with the peer's required-min-rx

chunks.title: timer: BFD Required Min Rx Interval
chunks.text: BFD Required Min Rx Interval timer for bfd. Set equal to tx-interval for symmetric sessions. Increasing required-min-rx tells the peer it may send less frequently, reducing CPU load on congested control planes

chunks.title: timer: LACP Actor Timeout (Short/Long)
chunks.text: LACP Actor Timeout (Short/Long) timer for lacp. Use SHORT (fast) timeout for LAG links to upstream spines where failure detection speed matters. LONG timeout reduces control-plane overhead on stable links. Both ends must agree on the timeout mode or one side will rapidly expire the other

chunks.title: timer: LACP PDU Transmission Interval
chunks.text: LACP PDU Transmission Interval timer for lacp. Fast rate is recommended for spine-leaf fabrics. Slow rate is acceptable for static or low-churn environments. The PDU interval is determined by the partner's timeout mode preference sent in the LACPDU

chunks.title: timer: LLDP Transmit Interval
chunks.text: LLDP Transmit Interval timer for lldp. 30s is the standard value per IEEE 802.1AB. Lower values (5–10s) are used in environments requiring faster topology discovery updates (e.g., automated provisioning systems). TTL is tx-interval * tx-hold

chunks.title: timer: LLDP Transmit Hold Multiplier
chunks.text: LLDP Transmit Hold Multiplier timer for lldp. Increasing tx-hold increases the TTL sent in LLDPDUs, giving neighbors more time before purging stale entries. Useful on flaky links. Do not set below 2

chunks.title: timer: LLDP Re-initialization Delay
chunks.text: LLDP Re-initialization Delay timer for lldp. Leave at default (2s) in most deployments. Increase only if observing LLDP churn during LAG reconvergence or STP topology changes

chunks.title: timer: STP/RSTP Hello Timer
chunks.text: STP/RSTP Hello Timer timer for rstp. Do not change the hello timer without adjusting forward-delay and max-age proportionally to satisfy the 802.1D stability criteria: 2*(forward-delay - 1) >= max-age, 2*(hello + 1) <= max-age. Mismatched timers cause topology instability. RSTP largely replaces time

chunks.title: timer: STP/RSTP Forward Delay
chunks.text: STP/RSTP Forward Delay timer for rstp. In pure RSTP environments, most ports converge in <1s via the proposal/agreement handshake and forward-delay is rarely the bottleneck. Tuning matters in mixed STP/RSTP environments or when RSTP rapid transition fails due to misconfiguration

chunks.title: timer: STP/RSTP Max Age
chunks.text: STP/RSTP Max Age timer for rstp. The relationship 2*(forward-delay - 1) >= max-age must hold. Reducing max-age below 20s risks spurious topology changes on congested control planes where BPDU delivery is delayed. In RSTP, rely on hello-interval and proposal/agreement rather than tuning max-age

chunks.title: timer: MC-LAG Keepalive Interval
chunks.text: MC-LAG Keepalive Interval timer for mclag. 1s is the recommended default. Lower values (sub-second) are not supported by SONiC iccpd. Higher values increase failover time. The keepalive link should be a dedicated L3 connection (not over the peer link) for independent failure detection

chunks.title: timer: MC-LAG Session Timeout
chunks.text: MC-LAG Session Timeout timer for mclag. Session timeout must be at least 3x the keepalive interval. 15s provides a balance between false-positive avoidance and fast failover. In stable environments with dedicated keepalive links, 10s is reasonable. Never set below 3s

chunks.title: database: APPL_DB
chunks.text: APPL_DB Redis database. Application desired state. *syncd daemons publish per-feature state here (routes, neighbors, ports, VLANs). Orchagent subscribes and translates entries into SAI calls. This is the boundary between feature daemons and the forwarding-plane pipeline

chunks.title: database: ASIC_DB
chunks.text: ASIC_DB Redis database. SAI object state mirroring what orchagent has programmed into the forwarding ASIC via libsai. Orchagent writes SAI-typed entries here; syncd reads them and makes the corresponding SAI API calls to the vendor SDK

chunks.title: database: COUNTERS_DB
chunks.text: COUNTERS_DB Redis database. Port-level and queue-level statistics counters polled from the ASIC via SAI. Populated by syncd at regular flex-counter intervals. Source of data for 'show interfaces counters' and Prometheus/telemetry exporters

chunks.title: database: LOGLEVEL_DB
chunks.text: LOGLEVEL_DB Redis database. Stores per-component log-level and log-output settings. Components read this at startup and watch for updates at runtime via a subscriber thread, enabling dynamic log-level changes without process restart

chunks.title: database: CONFIG_DB
chunks.text: CONFIG_DB Redis database. User configuration source of truth. All operator-configured state lives here: interfaces, IP addresses, VLANs, BGP neighbors, ACLs, QoS maps, port speeds. Loaded from /etc/sonic/config_db.json at boot via sonic-cfggen and updated live by CLI commands

chunks.title: database: FLEX_COUNTER_DB
chunks.text: FLEX_COUNTER_DB Redis database. Configuration for the flex-counter subsystem: which SAI object groups to poll, at what interval, and which stats to collect. Orchagent writes polling group definitions here; syncd reads them to schedule counter collection into COUNTERS_DB

chunks.title: database: STATE_DB
chunks.text: STATE_DB Redis database. Operational state used for cross-module dependencies and readiness gating. Daemons publish 'ready' flags and operational facts here (e.g., port operational status, LAG membership, interface admin state) that other daemons depend on before proceeding with configuration

chunks.title: database: SNMP_OVERLAY_DB
chunks.text: SNMP_OVERLAY_DB Redis database. Stores SNMP-specific overlay data: sysDescr, sysObjectID, and per-interface SNMP index mappings. The SNMP agent (sonic-snmpagent / snmpd) reads and writes here to serve SNMP MIB queries without polling ASIC_DB directly

chunks.title: database: RESTAPI_DB
chunks.text: RESTAPI_DB Redis database. Internal state store for the REST API server (sonic-restapi). Holds request-response context and auth token state needed by the REST API service to maintain session continuity

chunks.title: database: GB_ASIC_DB
chunks.text: GB_ASIC_DB Redis database. SAI object state for Gearbox (retimer/PHY) devices. Mirrors the role of ASIC_DB (db 1) but scoped to external Gearbox ASICs managed by the gearboxmgrd/gbsyncd subsystem

chunks.title: database: GB_COUNTERS_DB
chunks.text: GB_COUNTERS_DB Redis database. Port-level and queue-level statistics counters for Gearbox ASICs. Mirrors the role of COUNTERS_DB (db 2) scoped to Gearbox devices

chunks.title: database: GB_FLEX_COUNTER_DB
chunks.text: GB_FLEX_COUNTER_DB Redis database. Flex-counter polling configuration for Gearbox ASIC statistics. Mirrors the role of FLEX_COUNTER_DB (db 5) scoped to Gearbox devices

chunks.title: database: CHASSIS_APP_DB
chunks.text: CHASSIS_APP_DB Redis database. Multi-chassis desired state. On distributed chassis systems (e.g., Cisco 8800, Arista 7800R3), the supervisor publishes system-wide configuration and line-card state here so individual line-card SONiC instances can synchronize

chunks.title: database: CHASSIS_STATE_DB
chunks.text: CHASSIS_STATE_DB Redis database. Multi-chassis operational state. Line cards publish their operational facts (port status, fabric health, temperature, power) into this DB so the supervisor can aggregate system-level operational state

chunks.title: database: APPL_STATE_DB
chunks.text: APPL_STATE_DB Redis database. Read-only mirror of successfully applied application state. After orchagent successfully programs an APPL_DB entry into the ASIC, it publishes the confirmed state here. Allows consumers to distinguish 'desired' (APPL_DB) from 'applied' (APPL_STATE_DB) state without poll
